nav.subtitle: Characters, users, and memory workspace
nav.intro: Keep the core workflow usable first: manage users and models, organize nested cocoons, then continue the story in a dedicated chat page.

login.title: Welcome Back
login.description: Sign in to enter the Cocoon-SIT workspace and continue configuring characters, models, and nested cocoon conversations.
login.registerDescription: Register with an invite code and enter the Cocoon-SIT workspace right away.
login.error: Sign in failed. Please check your username and password.
login.registerError: Registration failed. Please verify the invite code and your input.
login.adminHint: The default admin account comes from the backend .env configuration.
login.registerHint: Registration requires an invite code and a password with at least 8 characters.
login.registerInviteHint: Successful registration creates the invite-tree relationship and signs you in immediately.

me.title: My Account
me.description: View the current session and update your profile.
me.overviewDescription: This shows the currently signed-in account and its capabilities.
me.profileDescription: Username changes take effect immediately. Password changes follow backend session logic.

users.title: User Management
users.description: Manage real user records exposed by the backend: username, email, role, activation state, and password rotation.
users.noPermission: This account does not have permission to manage users.
users.roleReferenceDescription: Available roles and permission summaries are shown here to make assignment faster.
users.dialogEditDescription: Update email, role, activation state, and optionally rotate the password.
users.dialogCreateDescription: Create a new backend user with an initial password and role assignment.
users.allowAudit: Allow this user to access the audit page

characters.title: Characters
characters.description: Manage character definitions and configure ACL for users, groups, subtrees, and all authenticated users.
characters.loginRequired: Please sign in before opening the character library.
characters.dialogDescription: Name and personality prompt directly affect Cocoon conversations.
characters.makePublic: Expose this character to authenticated users
characters.aclDialogDescription: Configure access levels for users, groups, subtrees, and all authenticated users.
characters.aclEmpty: There are no extra ACL rules yet.
characters.effectiveDescription: Inspect the final permission sources resolved by the backend for a user or group.
characters.effectiveEmpty: No effective access result loaded yet.
characters.permissionReadDesc: Can view character details and prompt data.
characters.permissionUseDesc: Can create cocoons from this character.
characters.permissionManageDesc: Can edit, delete, and manage ACL for this character.
characters.confirmDeleteDescription: Deletion is irreversible. The backend will reject it if a cocoon still uses this character.

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Manage hierarchical user groups, memberships, and group-level invite quota summaries.
groups.listDescription: This shows groups visible to the current account, including groups you own or have joined.
groups.noSelectionDescription: Select a group from the left to view its details and members.
groups.membersDescription: Add visible users to the current group.
groups.noMembers: This group has no members yet.
groups.dialogDescription: Groups support parent-child structure for quota distribution and permission inspection.

invites.title: Invites & Quotas
invites.description: Manage personal/group invite codes, quota grants, and current quota summaries.
invites.loadFailed: Failed to load invite and quota data
invites.groupSummaryFailed: Failed to load group quota summary
invites.summaryDescription: The top cards show the current user's invite quota and the selected group's quota.
invites.grantsDescription: Records every quota grant made to users or groups.
invites.codesDescription: Shows visible invite codes, their source, and their status.
invites.codeDialogDescription: Creating a code consumes quota immediately. Only unused codes can be revoked and refunded.
invites.grantDialogDescription: Grant your invite quota to an individual user or a whole group.

merges.title: Memory Merges
merges.description: Create merge jobs and inspect their execution trace in a structured UI.
merges.selectSourceTarget: Please choose both source and target cocoons
merges.sameSourceTarget: Source and target cocoons cannot be the same
merges.invalidLineage: The target cocoon must be the source cocoon itself or one of its ancestors
merges.createDescription: Choose source and target cocoons, then configure merge and dialogue handling options.
merges.includeDialogues: Include dialogue content in the merge
merges.listDescription: Inspect recent merge jobs and open detailed traces.
merges.detailDescription: Shows lifecycle, counters, and backend summary fields without inventing missing metadata.
merges.detailEmpty: Select a job on the left to inspect the structured detail view.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Control registration, default context limits, and the allowed model whitelist.
settings.noPermission: This account does not have permission to manage system settings.
settings.systemConfigDescription: These values are now stored in backend system settings and affect public features, cocoon defaults, and request debounce behavior.
settings.coreDescription: These values directly affect Cocoon defaults.
settings.allowRegistrationHint: When enabled, the login page exposes self-service registration backed by invite-code validation.
settings.defaultAutoCompactionHint: New cocoons inherit this switch unless the creator overrides it explicitly.
settings.compactMemoryHelp: This is the maximum number of compacted memories that may be written, not a fixed required count.
settings.modelWhitelistDescription: If no model is selected, there is no restriction. Once selected, only these models can be used to create cocoons.
settings.noModelsAvailable: No models are available yet. Sync provider models first.
settings.runtimeDescription: This page only shows maintenance and prompt capabilities that are actually backed by the backend.
settings.backendCapabilityDescription: These cards only show backend-backed capability signals, not local placeholders.
settings.promptTemplatesDescription: Runtime prompt templates are editable from the dedicated prompt settings page.
settings.unsupportedConfigNote: Mutable system settings and configuration-write APIs are not exposed by the current backend yet.
settings.preferencesDescription: These control the current browser's UI language and theme.
settings.languageDescription: Change the display language for this interface.
settings.themeDescription: Change the appearance theme in this browser.
settings.maintenanceQueueDescription: Queue durable maintenance jobs that the backend actually supports.
settings.maintenanceDescription: Run rollback cleanup immediately if you need to purge expired rollback tails.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Manage semantic tag definitions used by cocoons, messages, and long-term memory retrieval.
tags.dialogDescription: Tags define visibility and isolation boundaries across cocoon state, messages, and memories.
tags.confirmDeletePrompt: Delete tag "{{name}}" and scrub all related bindings?

prompts.title: Prompt Templates
prompts.description: Edit the global prompt stack used by runtime meta decisions, generation, memory compaction, pull, merge, and audit summaries.
prompts.noPermission: This account does not have permission to manage prompt templates.
prompts.resetConfirm: Reset {{name}} to the current system default template?
prompts.statusDescription: These templates are global and take effect on the next runtime round.

system.name: System Template
system.description: Global identity, tone, and hard constraints shared by all runtime rounds.

meta.name: Meta Template
meta.description: Pre-reply reasoning instructions for deciding whether and how the character should respond.

generator.name: Generator Template
generator.description: The main reply-generation template for ordinary chat rounds.

memory_summary.name: Memory Summary Template
memory_summary.description: Guides memory compaction and summary writing.

pull.name: Pull Template
pull.description: Guides pull summarization when one cocoon absorbs another cocoon's knowledge.

merge.name: Merge Template
merge.description: Guides merge summarization and reconciliation output.

audit_summary.name: Audit Summary Template
audit_summary.description: Summarizes completed runs for the audit chain.

prompts.dialogDescription: The editor saves a new immutable revision every time you update a template.
prompts.helperDescription: Click a variable to insert its placeholder into the template.

variables.character_settings: Structured character data, including role-specific prompt summary and settings.
variables.session_state: The current dynamic session state, such as relation score, persona patch, and active tags.
variables.visible_messages: Recent visible dialogue messages available to the current round.
variables.runtime_event: The current runtime event payload, such as chat, wakeup, pull, or merge.
variables.wakeup_context: Wakeup-specific context when the current round was triggered by a wakeup task.
variables.merge_context: Merge-specific context when the current round is processing a merge.
variables.provider_capabilities: Resolved model/provider capability data for the current round.

cocoons.title: Cocoons
cocoons.description: Manage nested cocoon structure here. Conversation, memory, and runtime details live in a dedicated workspace page.
cocoons.loadReferenceFailed: Failed to load characters or model data
cocoons.treeDescription: Parent-child relationships define inheritance paths. This page is for structure only, not chat.
cocoons.treeEmpty: There are no cocoons yet. Start by creating a root cocoon.
cocoons.selectedDescription: Inspect the real cocoon configuration returned by the backend before entering the workspace.
cocoons.roleInheritanceHint: Child cocoons inherit role settings from the parent chain unless explicitly overridden.
cocoons.workspaceHint: This page does not render chat. Use the button above to enter the dedicated workspace.
cocoons.emptySelection: Select a cocoon on the left to see structure details and available actions.
cocoons.usageLine1: Stay on this page if you are organizing structure. Enter the dedicated workspace if you want to chat, inspect memories, or manage the latest round.
cocoons.usageLine2: Keeping “inherit from parent” when creating a child cocoon is the easiest way to branch a story from the current node.
cocoons.dialogCreateChildDescription: A child cocoon can inherit the parent cocoon's character and model. If you keep “inherit from parent”, the backend will reuse parent settings automatically.
cocoons.dialogDefaultDescription: Fill in the base cocoon information and the runtime settings that are actually supported by the backend.
cocoons.confirmDeleteDescription: Deleting it will also clear related messages, memories, and audit records.

workspace.description: Continue the current cocoon like a normal AI chat page, with memory and runtime entry points on the right.
workspace.ignored: This input was ignored by the model
workspace.emptyMessages: No messages yet. Send the first message to begin this branch.
workspace.inputHint: Messages are written into the current cocoon and continue generation along the inherited parent chain.
workspace.memoriesDescription: Recent visible memories for the current cocoon are shown here so you can clean them up while chatting.
workspace.editDialogDescription: Saving here will regenerate the reply based on the updated user input.

audits.noPermission: This account does not have permission to view audit records.
audits.title: Audits
audits.description: Inspect runtime rounds, decisions, and trace structure by cocoon.
audits.roundsDescription: Select a round to see conversation, decisions, and trace details in structured UI blocks.
audits.detailDescription: Inspect the structured audit graph: summary metrics, steps, artifacts, and graph relations.
audits.runsDescription: Structured ledger entries for replies, silences, wakeups, pulls, and merges.
audits.timelineDescription: Unified audit, message, internal event, wakeup, and sync job timeline.
audits.traceEmpty: No trace fields available to display.
audits.detailEmpty: Select a round on the left to see structured summary, conversation, and trace details.

insights.title: Insights
insights.description: Global admin dashboard for memory, token usage, runtime behavior, and health.
insights.noPermission: This account does not have permission to view system insights.

tokenTrend.title: Token Trend
tokenTrend.description: Prompt and completion tokens over time.
